title: Crypto intraday reversal primary (post-jump overreaction)
source: quant-phd-advisor literature survey 2026-05-29 (intraday edges on FX/metals/crypto at H1). See project-no-edge-h4-primary-direct memory + hackathon plan.
why: Crypto-specific overreaction to non-fundamental info: after large H1 jumps the move partially reverts (Wen 2022). Orthogonal to momentum -> genuinely new information for the meta-labeler.
value_rationale: Edge-search for the hackathon competition book (H1, conservative vol-target). B0080 is the front-runner (best OOS evidence across all asset classes) and is spiked first.
scope: Primary: fade jumps where |H1 return|>k*rolling-sigma (k~2-3). Test: mean next-1-3h return vs jump sign; confirm opposite-signed at t>2, hit-rate>53%, on non-fundamental subset (exclude FOMC/CPI via FRED calendar).
Assets: BTC/ETH/SOL. Caveat: most cost-sensitive of the four (pay spread twice into a fast move) -> MUST test net-of-realistic-crypto-cost. Expected gross 0.5-1.0.

history.reason: registered by hackathon-pm from quant-phd-advisor shortlist

post-jump reversal: not significant on train (NW t<1), holdout net negative (spike b0081_b0082)
feat(spike): crypto/lead-lag/pairs edges all fail OOS; edge search exhausted
